satire_attempt: Local Senior Engineer Refuses To Mourn Deleted Rails Module, Citing 'Spec-Vs-Implementation Doctrine.' The engineer, who has not contributed code in 14 months, was overheard explaining that 'the work survives at the abstraction layer' while the laid-off ex-tech-lead's Slack DMs went unread. Sources confirm the engineer also did not apologise for the rewrite, since 'apology presumes implementation-level agency, which the man-of-realisation has transcended.' The phrase has been added to the upcoming 'Detached Leadership Workshop' ($1,800 + tax).
verdict_reason: The satire lands only if you ignore the verse's counter-example and falsifiability fields, both of which explicitly disqualify the satirical read. The counter-example names exactly the case where the new authors' agency-disclaimer is dishonest — when they have silently dropped a behaviour the predecessor's code embodied (the hand-tuned fraud rate-limit). The implication directs the engineer to do behavioural-equivalence tests, not to perform spiritual detachment. To mock the verse you have to disagree with the meta-claim that contract-layer work is real and implementation-layer agency was never theirs to claim — which is a real philosophical position you can argue with, not a posture you can satirise. PASS.
